targets.name: BAKER HUGHES DIGITAL SOLUTIONS AUSTRALIA PTY LTD

anzsic_codes.name: Other Professional and Scientific Equipment Manufacturing

merger_description:
Hexagon AB (**Hexagon**), through its subsidiary, Hexagon Manufacturing Intelligence LLC, is proposing to acquire the Waygate business, including certain assets of Baker Hughes Digital Solutions Australia Pty Ltd (together, **Waygate**).

Waygate is currently indirectly wholly owned and controlled by Baker Hughes Company and its subsidiary, Baker Hughes Holdings LLC (together, **Baker Hughes**).

In Australia, Hexagon is involved in the provision of non-destructive testing (**NDT**) solutions, including through the supply of its Volume Graphics software, which is used for industrial computed tomography-based non-destructive evaluation. This software enables the detection of internal defects and allows for dimensional measurement and material analysis without damaging components. Hexagon’s Volume Graphics software is primarily supplied to science and research companies.

Waygate is an industrial technology company specialising in NDT and inspection solutions used to assess the integrity, quality, and performance of materials, components, and industrial assets. Waygate's core business involves the development and supply of integrated hardware, software, and services that enable customers to detect defects, verify quality, and monitor the condition of critical assets throughout their lifecycle. Its offerings are centred on a range of NDT technologies that are broadly categorised as Radiography, Imaging Solutions, Remote Visual Inspection, and Ultrasonic Testing. These technologies are used to inspect both the internal and external characteristics of components, supporting applications such as dimensional measurement, defect detection, and structural analysis.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Hexagon AB (Hexagon), through its subsidiary Hexagon
Manufacturing Intelligence LLC, applied for a notification waiver in
respect of its proposed acquisition of the Waygate business,
including certain assets of Baker Hughes Digital Solutions Australia
Pty Ltd (together, Waygate), as described in the transaction
documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The acquirer, Hexagon, is a multinational industrial technology
company headquartered in Stockholm. Together with its
subsidiaries, Hexagon supplies a range of measuring technology and
geospatial tools and software. In Australia, Hexagon is involved in
the provision of non-destructive testing (NDT) solutions, including
through the supply of its Volume Graphics software. This software is
used for industrial computed tomography-based non-destructive
evaluation, to enable the detection of internal defects, dimensional
measurement, and material analysis without damaging components.
The target, Waygate, is currently indirectly wholly owned and
controlled by Baker Hughes Company and its subsidiary, Baker
Hughes Holdings LLC.
Waygate is an industrial technology company specialising in NDT
and inspection solutions used to assess the integrity, quality, and
performance of materials, components, and industrial assets. Its
core business involves the development and supply of integrated
hardware, software, and services that enable customers to detect
defects, verify quality, and monitor the condition of critical assets
through their lifecycle. Waygate’s range of NDT technologies are
used to inspect both the internal and external characteristics of
components, supporting applications such as dimensional
measurement, defect detection, and structural analysis.
The parties are both global providers of NDT solutions.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors
in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010
(Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. there is limited overlap between the parties in the supply
of NDT solutions and software in Australia, as their
respective offerings primarily serve different use cases
b. there are alternative providers of the types of NDT
solutions and software offered by the parties available in
Australia
c. there appears to be a low risk of foreclosure or other
concerning vertical or conglomerate effects resulting
from exclusionary conduct, bundling or tying of products
post-acquisition.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
